name: Running - Circular to Sant Jordi Hermitage from Camarasa
name_original: Running - Circular a la Ermita de Sant Jordi desde Camarasa
region_intro: Jogging routes around Camarasa traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Segre River and its dramatic gorges, situated between the Carbonera and Mont-roig mountain ranges. The region features the expansive Camarasa reservoir, offering routes with water views. Mountainous terrain, where the Central Depression meets the Pre-Pyrenees, provides varied elevation and panoramic vistas for runners. This environment includes peaks and ranges like the Serra del Mont-roig and Montsec, contributing to a network of trails suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Camarasa The most popular jogging route is Running - Circular Cima del Monteró from San Lorenzo de Montgai, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a significant elevation gain of over 1300 feet (398 meters). Another top favourite among local runners is Running - Tour of the Mu Gorge — Confluence of the Segre and the Noguera Pallaresa, a difficult 14.1 miles (22.6 km) path. This route follows the dramatic Mu Gorge and the confluence of two rivers, offering a challenging running experience. Local runners also love the Running - Circular to Sant Jordi Hermitage from Camarasa, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain to the Sant Jordi Hermitage, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Jogging around Camarasa is defined by river paths along the Segre, dramatic gorges, and mountainous trails in the Pre-Pyrenees. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Camarasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Camarasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Camarasa?
A: Camarasa offers a diverse network of over 50 running routes. These include 2 easy paths, 10 moderate trails, and 44 more challenging options, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly running paths in Camarasa?
A: Yes, Camarasa features a few easier running paths suitable for beginners or families. While most routes are moderate to difficult, there are 2 easy trails available. The riverside paths along the Segre River, as mentioned in the region's characteristics, often provide scenic and less strenuous options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Camarasa?
A: The running trails in Camarasa offer a varied terrain. You can expect river paths along the Segre, dramatic gorges, and mountainous trails in the Pre-Pyrenees. The region is characterized by its natural beauty, with routes traversing the Carbonera and Mont-roig mountain ranges, and often providing views of the Camarasa reservoir.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Camarasa?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Camarasa are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931289229/running-circular-to-sant-jordi-hermitage-from-camarasa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running - Circular to Sant Jordi Hermitage from Camarasa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 10.8 km trail leading through varied terrain.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints I can see while running in Camarasa?
A: Camarasa's routes offer stunning natural attractions and viewpoints. You can run past the dramatic Congost de Camarasa, enjoy expansive views of the Camarasa reservoir, or explore mountainous areas like the Serra del Mont-roig and Montsec. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1042597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jordi Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers excellent views of Camarasa and the Segre River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1045778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pala Alta Summit&lt;/a&gt; also provides panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, you can combine your run with exploring the region's heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova del Tabac&lt;/a&gt;, a monumental cave with ancient cave paintings, is accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930462460/running-circular-route-of-the-cueva-del-tabaco-sierra-del-mont-roig" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running - Circular route of the Cueva del Tabaco — Sierra del Mont-roig&lt;/a&gt;. The church of Sant Martí (18th century) and the Raiers Interpretation Centre are also notable attractions in the area.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Camarasa?
A: The running routes in Camarasa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Camarasa's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from river paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the rewarding views.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Camarasa offers several challenging long-distance routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931093253/running-tour-of-the-mu-gorge-confluence-of-the-segre-and-the-noguera-pallaresa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running - Tour of the Mu Gorge — Confluence of the Segre and the Noguera Pallaresa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.6 km path with significant elevation gain, following the dramatic Mu Gorge.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Camarasa?
A: Camarasa is suitable for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. The diverse landscape means you can find pleasant conditions in spring and autumn for cooler runs, while summer mornings or evenings are ideal to avoid the heat. Winter offers a different, often crisp, experience, especially on lower-elevation trails.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by the Camarasa reservoir?
A: Yes, the presence of the Camarasa reservoir means there are routes that offer expansive, often flatter, perimeters ideal for jogging with picturesque water views. While not explicitly listed in the top routes, many trails in the region will provide glimpses or direct access to the reservoir's scenic surroundings.

Q: Can I find parking near the trailheads in Camarasa?
A: Given Camarasa's popularity as an outdoor activities hub, parking facilities are generally available near trailheads and in the village itself. It's advisable to check specific route starting points for the most convenient parking options.

Q: Are there any specific routes with significant elevation gain for trail runners?
A: Absolutely. Camarasa is located where the Central Depression meets the Pre-Pyrenees, offering a mountainous environment with various peaks. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931100274/torre-de-sant-salvador-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Sant Salvador Loop&lt;/a&gt; feature over 570 meters of elevation gain, appealing to experienced trail runners seeking a challenge.
tips: Good views of Camarasa and the Segre river. The hermitage of Sant Jordi was built in the 19th century to replace a previous temple in ruins that can be found half a km from the current one to the north. The interior is simple with an image of Sant Jordi
